key.focus_hint_normalized: docs openspec papers continue keystone neurons causal importance guided vit pruning

result:
**Research value: high** -- Recent primary work establishes both causal pruning and ViT activation patching, but not their combination for hardware-realized, interaction-aware structured ViT compression and anomaly-transfer preservation.

### Prior Art
- **CAP** already uses per-head interventional importance to guide pruning, so “causal head importance for pruning” is not novel. Its scope is LLM reasoning and maps head scores to fine-grained weight pruning; it reports gains over Wanda at moderate sparsity. Keystone must claim the **ViT, structured-head, and representation-transfer** setting. [CAP, 27 Apr 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.19350)
- **CC-Prune** is direct conceptual prior art: activation-patching scores protect a causal circuit during magnitude pruning. It is an unpublished, zero-star NLP/JSTS repository, uses neuron/head scoring, and does not establish ViT or structured acceleration. Treat it as a mandatory baseline/reproduction threat, not evidence of field consensus. [Official repository, accessed 13 Jul 2026](https://github.com/ikari12/Causal_Pruning)
- **Vi-CD** provides ViT edge-circuit discovery, including faithful visual interventions and class-specific circuits, but stops before compression. Keystone’s clean boundary is: *does a faithful circuit score remain a useful **structured removal** score across ratios and downstream tasks?* [15 Apr 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.14477)
- **CAAP** is a major 2026 novelty risk: it uses activation patching inside ViTs for causal **image-patch** attribution. It does not rank heads or prune, but invalidates any broad claim that activation patching in ViTs is new. [13 Mar 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.13652)
- ViT pruning is no longer a weak baseline space. **SnapViT** performs label-free, retraining-free structured pruning on DINO/SigLIP/DeiT-style models; **LPViT** demonstrates hardware-aware block pruning; **Isomorphic Pruning** argues importance must be ranked within comparable structural groups. Compare against at least SnapViT-style self-supervised structure scoring and per-layer/isomorphic ranking, and report measured latency rather than FLOPs alone. [SnapViT, 18 Sep 2025](https://openreview.net/forum?id=OU6FXkSIe0&noteId=dtxFrUanZF), [LPViT, 2 Jul 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.02068), [Isomorphic Pruning, 5 Jul 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.04616)

### Adjacent Solutions
- **Interaction-aware causal ranking is the strongest contribution direction.** Individual activation-patching scores include component interactions; greedy rankings can hide or inflate heads whose effect is conditional on other heads. Add pairwise tests for the top/bottom causal heads and report an interaction index. This makes “Keystone” about preserving *redundant yet jointly load-bearing groups*, not merely ranking individual heads. [Multiple Mediators, 25 Jun 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27510)
- **Faithfulness/stability is a publishable result even without accuracy wins.** Test rank stability across corruption pairs, metrics, layers, and image subsets; then test whether causal rank predicts actual post-pruning loss better than magnitude, Taylor, and gradient ranks. Gradient rankings can invert causal relevance under redundancy, but this evidence is currently limited to algorithmic transformers, so frame it as a hypothesis to test in ViTs. [Hidden Heroes, 1 Feb 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.01442)
- **Scale through verified screening.** Use a fast candidate ranker, then activation-patch only candidates around the decision boundary. RelP/AtP* motivate speed-faithfulness comparisons, but must be validated against exact ViT patching rather than assumed transferable from LLMs. [RelP, 28 Aug 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.21258), [AtP*, 1 Mar 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.00745)

### Cross-Domain Analogies
- **Reliability engineering:** individual head removal is a single-component failure test; structured pruning is a multi-component failure. A component can appear noncritical alone while being essential in a redundant set. Report single-head and group-ablation survival curves.
- **Anomaly detection transfer:** DINO features are already used in training-free nearest-neighbor AD, but MVTec AD has saturated. Use MVTec AD 2 or an explicit illumination/defect-shift split as the transfer stress test; measure AU-PRO and low-FPR recall, not only AUROC. [MVTec AD 2, 27 Mar 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.21622), [SuperAD, 26 May 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.19750)

### Sources
- [CAP, 27 Apr 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.19350) — causal head attribution driving LLM weight pruning.
- [Vi-CD, 15 Apr 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.14477) — faithful ViT edge-circuit discovery.
- [CAAP, 13 Mar 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.13652) — ViT internal activation patching for image-patch attribution.
- [Multiple Mediators, 25 Jun 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.27510) — interaction bias in activation-patching rankings.
- [SnapViT, 18 Sep 2025](https://openreview.net/forum?id=OU6FXkSIe0&noteId=dtxFrUanZF) — current retraining-free structured ViT pruning baseline.
- [MVTec AD 2, 27 Mar 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.21622) — tougher anomaly-transfer benchmark.
